title: Noccaea cariensis
intro: Noccaea cariensis, also known as Turkish mountain savory, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white, or purple flowers and edible leaves. The leaves of Noccaea cariensis have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Noccaea cariensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Noccaea cariensis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which may be used to treat respiratory issues, and also as an ingredient in herbal syrups and cough medicines.
taxonomy: Noccaea cariensis is classified within the family Brassicaceae. This family is commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. The genus Noccaea is a group of flowering plants that are typically found in areas with alkaline soils. Noccaea cariensis is native to Turkey. It is closely related to other species within the Brassicaceae family, such as those found in the genera Alyssum and Alyssoides.
